title: Incident Database — Methodology & Inclusion Criteria
lede: How incidents are selected, classified, and described in the RWA-Index Tokenization Incident Database.

purpose.title: 1. Purpose
purpose.p1: The Tokenization Incident Database is a structured factual record of incidents affecting stablecoins, tokenized real-world assets, and tokenization infrastructure. Its purpose is academic: to provide researchers, practitioners, and policymakers with a systematically organised reference for understanding historical failure modes in tokenized asset markets.
purpose.p2: The database does not investigate, accuse, or make legal determinations. All entries are based on publicly available information including regulatory filings, court records, official statements, and reporting from established media outlets. Where facts are disputed, the database records the dispute and does not resolve it.
purpose.p3: The unique analytical contribution of this database is the mapping of each incident to the SARM (stablecoin) and RARM (tokenized RWA) analytical frameworks developed by RWA-Index. This mapping identifies which risk dimensions were implicated in each event, enabling cross-incident pattern analysis.

hk.title: HK-Related Incidents
hk.intro: Included if the incident involves any of:
bullets: An issuer or operator licensed, incorporated, or operating from Hong Kong
A distributor or service provider targeting Hong Kong residents
Regulatory action by the SFC, HKMA, or HK Police
Reported losses by Hong Kong residents or institutions
A Hong Kong regulatory precedent or policy change resulting from the incident
hk.note: No severity threshold — all severities included with HK nexus.

global.title: Global Reference Incidents
global.intro: Included only if the incident meets at least one of:
bullets: Demonstrably shifted industry practice or regulatory approach in two or more jurisdictions
global.note: Global incidents without HK nexus are included for comparative context only.

severityRubric.title: 3. Severity Rubric
severityRubric.intro: Severity is assigned based on the highest applicable threshold. Where impact is estimated from a range, the upper bound is used unless a single verified figure is available from regulatory or court records.

definitions.de-pegging: A stablecoin or pegged asset trades materially and persistently below its target peg on secondary markets. Includes both algorithmic and fiat-backed stablecoins.
definitions.smart-contract-exploit: Funds are lost or misappropriated through a vulnerability in a smart contract or on-chain protocol, including bridges, vaults, and liquidity pools.
definitions.custody-incident: Assets held in custody by an exchange, custodian, or bridge are lost, misappropriated, or made inaccessible through operational, security, or governance failure.
definitions.redemption-failure: A token or instrument issuer is unable or unwilling to honour redemption requests at par value within the committed timeframe.
definitions.regulatory-action: A regulatory body takes enforcement, licensing, or supervisory action against an issuer, platform, or individual that materially disrupts operations or imposes financial penalties.
definitions.governance-failure: An incident arising from failures in internal governance, including board dysfunction, management misconduct, inadequate internal controls, or fraud by principals.
definitions.bank-failure-spillover: An incident in a traditional banking institution that causes secondary stress to a tokenized asset or stablecoin, for example through custody concentration or reserve impairment.
definitions.sanctions: A regulatory or governmental sanctions designation that disrupts the operation or use of a tokenized asset, stablecoin, or associated infrastructure.
definitions.other: Incidents not clearly classifiable under the above types. The specific nature is described in the incident entry.

definitions.resolved: The incident has concluded and all regulatory or legal proceedings are finalised.
definitions.ongoing: The incident is actively developing; operations or recovery efforts are still in progress.
definitions.under-investigation: The incident is under active investigation by a regulator or law enforcement body. No charges or findings yet.
definitions.litigation: Civil or criminal proceedings are actively underway; no final judgment or settlement has been reached.
definitions.settled: Proceedings have concluded through a settlement, consent judgment, or plea agreement. The case is closed.

sourceHierarchy.title: 6. Source Hierarchy
sourceHierarchy.intro: Sources are ranked in the following priority order. Lower-tier sources are used only when higher-tier sources are unavailable for a specific claim. All claims requiring a source include a direct citation.

tierExamples.1: SFC enforcement notices, OFAC designations, SEC complaints, court indictments
tierExamples.2: Bankruptcy filings, judgments, settlement orders, indictments
tierExamples.3: Issuer press releases, company blog posts, protocol post-mortems
tierExamples.4: Reuters, Bloomberg, Financial Times, South China Morning Post
tierExamples.5: CoinDesk, The Block, Decrypt — for facts not covered in higher-tier sources

updatePolicy.title: 7. Update Policy
updatePolicy.p1: Entries are updated when new public information becomes available, including regulatory findings, court judgments, revised loss estimates from official proceedings, or corrections to previously published figures.
updatePolicy.p2: All material revisions are recorded in the entry's revision notes field with a date and description. Prior text is not deleted; the current entry reflects the most accurate publicly available information.
updatePolicy.p3: Where loss estimates are revised by courts or regulators, the database uses the most recent verified figure and notes the revision. Earlier estimates are preserved in revision notes for traceability.

scopeLimitations.title: 8. Scope Limitations
bullets: Investigate incidents beyond publicly available information
Allege wrongdoing beyond what is established in cited public sources
Make predictions about ongoing proceedings ("will likely be found guilty")
Consolidate multiple distinct events into a single entry — each major event has its own slug
Include rumoured or unverified loss figures; uncertain figures are described as estimates with source notes
Include incidents below the global threshold unless they have an HK nexus

submissions.title: 9. Submissions & Corrections
submissions.body: To submit a new incident for consideration, or to flag a factual error or update in an existing entry, please email <email>research@rwa-index.com</email> with the subject line "Incident Database: [submission/correction]". Please include the incident name or slug, the specific claim in question, and a link to the public source supporting the correction.

severityThresholds.critical: ≥ USD 1 billion impact OR systemic financial system effect
severityThresholds.high: USD 100 million – 1 billion impact OR multi-jurisdictional regulatory action
severityThresholds.medium: USD 10 million – 100 million impact OR formal single-jurisdiction enforcement action
severityThresholds.low: Below USD 10 million impact, contained, no formal enforcement action
